Jalisco New Generation Cartel.description: Mexican criminal organization dedicated to drug trafficking and arms smuggling, designated as a foreign terrorist organization by the U.S. and Argentina. Founded in 2007 as an armed wing called Los Mata Zetas, it became independent in 2010 and emerged publicly in 2011. Led by Nemesio Oseguera Cervantes ("El Mencho") from 2009 until his death in 2026, it is considered one of Mexico's most dangerous cartels and a rival of the Sinaloa Cartel.

'Ndrina Farao.description: The 'Ndrina Farao is a powerful 'ndrina of the Calabrian 'Ndrangheta based in Cirò and Cirò Marina, allied with the Marincola. It operates in the provinces of Crotone and Cosenza, with groups in central and northern Italy and abroad in Germany. Its main activities are extortion and drug trafficking, with connections to Albanian mafia for drug and prostitution. The organization has been active since at least the 1970s and remains influential.

Los Cachiros.description: Honduran drug trafficking organization founded in the early 1990s by cattle ranchers in Colón and Olancho departments. It became a major cocaine transporter between Colombian and Mexican cartels, amassing a net worth of nearly one billion dollars. The group had extensive business and political ties, including bribing high-level Honduran officials. Its leaders surrendered to US authorities in 2015, leading to asset seizures.

Bala na Cara.description: Bala na Cara is a Brazilian criminal organization based in Porto Alegre, Rio Grande do Sul, founded in the early 2000s. It is one of the two largest and most violent criminal groups in the state, involved in drug trafficking, robberies, and murders. The group is known for extreme violence, often executing victims with multiple shots to the face. It has allied with Comando Vermelho and expanded operations to other states and countries.

D-Company.description: D-Company is an organized crime syndicate founded and controlled by Dawood Ibrahim, based in India. It was established in the 1970s by Dawood Ibrahim and his brother Shabir Ibrahim Kaskar. The syndicate is involved in murder, extortion, drug trafficking, and terrorism, and has been designated as a Global Terrorist Organization by the U.S. Department of the Treasury. It remains active, with operations across Asia, the Middle East, and North America.

'Ndrina Bellocco.description: The Bellocco 'ndrina is a powerful clan of the 'Ndrangheta based in Rosarno, Calabria, Italy, founded in the 1960s by Umberto Bellocco. It is heavily involved in drug trafficking, arms trafficking, extortion, and control of commercial activities, particularly in the Gioia Tauro plain. The clan has a strong presence in northern Italy, especially in Varese, and has been involved in setting up the Sacra Corona Unita in Apulia. Despite arrests of its leaders, the clan remains active.

descriptions: The feud ended with a ceasefire agreement between the two families.
edges.evidence_quote: The feud ended when the Qarajahs were forced by the government to have a cease fire agreement whereby they will leave Ramla to settle in any other Arab settlement.

'Ndrina Serraino.description: The Serraino are a powerful 'ndrina (organized crime family) of the 'Ndrangheta based in Cardeto, Santo Stefano in Aspromonte, and Reggio Calabria, Italy. Originating in the 19th century as part of the 'picciotteria' association, they have monopolized criminal activities in their home territory and expanded across Italy and internationally. Their illicit activities include drug and arms trafficking, with connections in Belgium, Canada, Bolivia, France, United States, Germany, Switzerland, Morocco, Libya, and Syria. They were considered the richest and most powerful family in Lombardy until the early 2000s.
